rulings.Case Title: **Transferred to Contra Costa County** MARIANA GREUB ET AL VS. AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC., ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR for Friday, Feb-13-2026, LINE 1. DEFENDANT AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Off calendar in light of the court's order transferring venue. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: UNIFIED ACCOUNTING & TAX, LLP, A CALIFORNIA VS. SHIFT TECHNOLOGIES, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Second Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR for Friday, Feb-13-2026, LINE 3. PLAINTIFF UNIFIED ACCOUNTING & TAX, LLP's, A CALIFORNIA LIMITIED LIABILITY COMPANY, MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Second Amended Complaint. Plaintiff's motion for leave to file a second amended complaint is granted. Plaintiff shall file the document attached as Exhibit 2 to the Lowe Declaration within two weeks. The court exercises great liberality in permitting amendment of the pleadings. (See Nestle v. Santa Monica (1972) 6 Cal.3d 920, 939.) The court may deny amendment where there is undue delay and prejudice. (Magpali v. Farmers Group, Inc. (1996) 48 Cal.App.4th 471, 487.) It may also deny amendment where a proposed claim is plainly futile. (Singh v. Lipworth (2014) 227 Cal.App.4th 813, 828.) Typically, however, the better way to test a pleading is through a pleadings motion after amendment. Here, there is no undue delay and defendant shows no prejudice. The trial date has not been set and there is ample time for discovery on the new allegations and cause of action. Defendant contends that the cause of action fails to state a claim. The pleading is not so plainly defective that leave to amend should be denied; rather, defendant may make its arguments (which the court does not pass on here) as appropriate once the pleading has been filed. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: AMY DHALL VS. KETCH KLOUD, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Jim Wangler To Provide Further Responses To Special Interrogatories Set No. One And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $7,500 Against Defendant Jim Wangler And His Counsel Neda N. Dal Cielo And Veena Bhatia
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR for Friday, Feb-13-2026, LINE 4. 1-PLAINTIFF AMY DHALL's Motion To Compel Defendant Jim Wangler To Provide Further Responses To Special Interrogatories Set No. One And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $7,500 Against Defendant Jim Wangler And His Counsel Neda N. Dal Cielo And Veena Bhatia. Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to her special interrogatories, set one, from defendant Jim Wangler, is denied. Plaintiff does not include a compliant separate statement or competent evidence of Wangler's discovery responses in her opening papers. The court further concludes that Plaintiff failed to satisfy her meet and confer obligation. Defendant seeks sanctions. The court concludes that the totality of circumstances would make the imposition of a sanction unjust and does not award sanctions. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: RAMESES JOHNSON VS. AMERICAN PRECISION BUILDERS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Louis Cherry To Respond To Form Interrogatories Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of 3277 Against Defendant Louis Cherry Memorandum Of Points And Authorities Declaration Of Charles Pressman Proposed Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR for Friday, Feb-13-2026, LINE 6. 2-PLAINTIFF RAMESES JOHNSON's Motion To Compel Defendant Louis Cherry To Respond To Form Interrogatories Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of 3277 Against Defendant Louis Cherry. The unopposed motion of plaintiff Rameses Johnson for responses to form interrogatories, set one, is granted. Johnson shows good cause; the discovery was served and defendant Louis Cherry has not responded. Defendant is ordered to serve complete, objection-free, verified responses by February 20, 2026. (CCP 2030.290(a) [waiver of objections by untimely response].) Plaintiff shall give notice. Plaintiff seeks sanctions. CCP 2030.290(c) provides that "the court shall impose a monetary sanction . . . against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel a response to interrogatories . . . unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." Sanctions are appropriate on a discovery motion where the objecting party does not act with substantial justification in its discovery compliance, even where the party does not oppose the motion. (See Rule of Court 3.1348(a).) The court does not find substantial justification and orders defendant to pay $2000 in sanctions to plaintiff by March 13, 2026. The court reduces the amount in the exercise of its discretion and because no reply brief was required. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
